sourceMovie.title: Vivo
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Vivo (2021) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kirk DeMicco's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Vivo clicked for you, Up (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Vivo?
faq.answer: Try Up, Sing, The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge on the Run—each shares DNA with Vivo while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Vivo?
faq.answer: Vivo (2021) is best described as Animation, Family, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Vivo worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
